proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall objective_response rate in the cohort is non-trivial (>5%) so that response prediction is feasible.

analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate is 16.9% (8452 / 50000). Cohort is large enough and event rate adequate for regression with many covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature feature_057 (levels 0, 1, 2) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response (negative association).

analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic dose-response: response rate 21.3% at feature_057=0, 15.5% at feature_057=1, 11.2% at feature_057=2. Logistic regression gives OR=0.681 per unit increase (95% CI ~0.66-0.71).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_011 (range 0-23.6) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Higher feature_011 reduces response. Logistic OR=0.961 per unit (beta=-0.0401). Mean feature_011 is 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders. Quartile dose-response: Q1=18.7%, Q4=13.7% response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_006 (range 20-100) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic OR=0.994 per unit (beta=-0.00574). Quartile means: Q1=19.0%, Q4=14.7% response. Significant negative monotonic trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 (a binary indicator with prevalence ~7.2%) have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_035=0.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive association: response rate 22.1% in feature_035=1 vs 16.5% in feature_035=0. Univariate OR=1.44 (beta=0.364). Behaves like a positive biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_063 (heavily right-skewed, range 0.03-300) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Significant negative association: OR=0.993 per unit (beta=-0.00702). Top decile shows the lowest response rate (14.6%) vs middle deciles (~17-18%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_099 (narrow range 1.7-5.5, mean ~3.8) are associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Significant positive association: OR=1.122 per unit (beta=+0.115). Decile response rates rise monotonically from ~15.6% (lowest deciles) to ~17.9% (highest deciles).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_092 (right-skewed, range 0.5-500) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Significant negative association: OR=0.998 per unit (beta=-0.00224). Mean is 19.1 in responders vs 20.4 in non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_093=1 (binary, prevalence ~8.0%) have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_093=0.

analyses.result_summary: Modest positive association: response 18.5% with feature_093=1 vs 16.8% with feature_093=0. Univariate OR=1.13 (beta=0.119).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_121=1 (binary, prevalence ~8.3%) have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_121=0.

analyses.result_summary: Modest negative association: response 15.6% in feature_121=1 vs 17.0% in feature_121=0. Univariate OR=0.904 (beta=-0.101).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across categories of feature_005 (5 levels: white, hispanic, black, asian, other), with white patients having the highest response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Significant variation by feature_005 (chi-square p=0.0096). Response rates: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. Vs white reference, black OR=0.896 (p=0.004), other OR=0.826 (p=0.034); hispanic and asian not significantly different.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across categories of feature_087 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: No significant difference: response medicaid 16.9%, medicare 17.2%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. All pairwise contrasts vs private have p>0.15. Insurance status is not associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92) is associated with the probability of objective_response, with older patients having lower response rates.

analyses.result_summary: No association: OR=1.0004 per year (beta=0.0004), p=0.71. Decile response rates are flat between 16.2% and 17.8% across the entire age range. Age does NOT predict response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (binary, prevalence 44.7%, plausibly a sex indicator) is associated with the probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: No association: response 17.1% in feature_007=1 vs 16.8% in feature_007=0; OR=1.025 (beta=0.025), p=0.32.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal features feature_042, feature_045, feature_064, feature_096, feature_125, and feature_018 (each with 3-11 ordered levels) are independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in [feat list]: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {c}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate ORs per level: feature_042 OR=0.990 p=0.35; feature_045 OR=0.981 p=0.07; feature_064 OR=1.014 p=0.21; feature_096 OR=1.007 p=0.51; feature_125 OR=0.977 p=0.038 (negative); feature_018 OR=1.004 p=0.34. Only feature_125 reaches univariate significance, and its effect attenuates to p=0.056 after adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006. Most ordinal features carry no signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all univariately significant predictors are entered jointly into a multivariable logistic regression, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_093, and feature_005 each retain independent association with objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035 + feature_063 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_093 + C(feature_005, Treatment(reference="white")) + feature_121', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All retain independent significance with directions matching univariate: feature_057 (OR per level ~0.68, p<1e-50), feature_011 (OR=0.96, p<1e-30), feature_006 (OR=0.994, p<1e-19), feature_035 (OR=1.43, p<1e-16), feature_063 (OR=0.993, p<1e-5), feature_099 (OR=1.12, p<1e-5), feature_092 (OR=0.998, p<1e-4), feature_093 (OR=1.13, p=0.003), feature_121 (OR=0.916, p=0.05). Race[black] OR=0.90 (p=0.005), race[other] OR=0.82 (p=0.030) vs white.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The boost in objective_response associated with feature_035=1 is larger in patients with low feature_057 (level 0) than in patients with high feature_057 (level 2), i.e. feature_057 modifies the feature_035 effect.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_057 * feature_035', df).fit()  # LR test vs main effects
analyses.result_summary: No interaction: likelihood-ratio test p=0.67. The OR for feature_035 is essentially constant across feature_057 levels. Effects are additive on the log-odds scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035=1 on objective_response differs by race (feature_005), e.g. is smaller in non-white patients than in white patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005) * feature_035', df).fit()  # LR test 4df
analyses.result_summary: No interaction: LR test 4df p=0.73. Stratified response rates show ~5 percentage-point uplift from feature_035=1 across all race groups (e.g. white 16.8%->23.3%; black 15.4%->19.5%; hispanic 16.4%->20.5%). The biomarker-like effect of feature_035 does not differ by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response is stronger in patients with high feature_057 (level 2) than in patients with low feature_057 (level 0).

analyses.result_summary: No interaction: LR test p=0.71. The slope of the feature_011 effect does not vary across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078) modifies the feature_057 effect on objective_response, such that the gap in response between feature_057=0 and feature_057=2 is wider in older patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 * C(feature_057)', df).fit()  # LR test 2df
analyses.result_summary: Borderline interaction: LR test 2df p=0.022. Stratifying by feature_057, age has no effect at level 0 (p=0.86) or level 1 (p=0.36) but is positively associated with response within feature_057=2 (beta=+0.0088 per year, p=0.009). The interaction is statistically significant but practically modest, since age has no main effect overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The black-vs-white disparity in objective_response is similar across feature_057 strata (no race x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: No race x feature_057 interaction: LR test 8df p=0.91. Black-vs-white response gap is consistent across strata (e.g. ECOG=0: 20.5% vs 21.7%; ECOG=1: 14.3% vs 15.8%; ECOG=2: 9.5% vs 11.7%). The disparity is uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all significant clinical predictors and insurance, black race (feature_005=='black') is associated with lower odds of objective_response vs white race; i.e. the race effect is not entirely explained by other measured covariates.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005) + C(feature_087) + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035 + feature_063 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_093 + feature_121', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Black vs white adjusted OR=0.896 (beta=-0.110, 95% CI -0.187 to -0.033, p=0.005). Other vs white adjusted OR=0.822 (p=0.030). Hispanic and asian not significantly different vs white. Insurance categories are uniformly null (all p>0.5). The race disparity for black patients persists after adjusting for clinical features and insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a monotonic dose-response in objective_response across quartiles of feature_011 and feature_006 (lower-quartile patients respond more than upper-quartile patients).

analyses.result_summary: Both show clear monotonic decline. feature_011 quartiles: Q1=18.7%, Q2=18.4%, Q3=16.9%, Q4=13.7% (Q1-Q4 absolute gap = 4.9 pts). feature_006 quartiles: Q1=19.0%, Q2=17.5%, Q3=16.5%, Q4=14.7% (gap = 4.3 pts). Both linear logistic effects are highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable logistic regression using all confirmed predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_093, feature_121, feature_005) achieves discriminative AUC > 0.55 for objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Final multivariable model AUC = 0.598 on the training cohort (apparent). Univariate AUCs: feature_057=0.566, feature_011=0.539, feature_006=0.532, feature_092=0.521, feature_099=0.517, feature_063=0.514, feature_035=0.514, feature_005=0.510. The composite improves on the strongest single predictor by ~3 percentage points but most patient-level variation in objective_response remains unexplained by these covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The set of features positively associated with objective_response (raise probability of response when higher / present) consists of feature_035, feature_099, and feature_093; the set negatively associated consists of feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_121, and (relative to white) the black and other categories of feature_005. Patient demographics feature_078 (age), feature_007, and feature_087 (insurance) carry no independent signal.
Effects of clinical predictors on objective_response are largely additive on the log-odds scale (no clinically meaningful interactions identified among the leading predictors).

analyses.code: summary table from final multivariable logistic regression
analyses.result_summary: Sign of effect estimates in final multivariable model: NEGATIVE (lower response) - feature_057 (beta=-0.384/level), feature_011 (-0.040), feature_006 (-0.0057), feature_063 (-0.0073), feature_092 (-0.0022), feature_121 (-0.088), feature_005[black] (-0.110), feature_005[other] (-0.196). POSITIVE (higher response) - feature_035 (+0.360), feature_099 (+0.118), feature_093 (+0.126). NULL - feature_078, feature_007, feature_087, and most other ordinal features.

analyses.code: all pairwise interaction LR tests among top predictors
analyses.result_summary: Of 8 interaction tests among leading predictors, only feature_078 x feature_057 reached p<0.05 (p=0.022) and that effect was modest. All other tested interactions (feature_057 x feature_035, feature_057 x feature_011, feature_057 x feature_006, feature_005 x feature_035, feature_005 x feature_057, feature_087 x feature_057, feature_007 x feature_057) had p>0.3. Effects therefore appear additive.
